body.title: Build EvaluatorCore — single-call evaluator with optimism parameter
body.result: EvaluatorCore built with optimism parameter; EvaluatorDevice._run_judge delegates to it; 41 tests pass
body.intention: I intend that a shared EvaluatorCore module exists that runs a single inference evaluation call with an optimism parameter, so Critic and Improver stop maintaining their own inference logic.
body.description:
Critic, Improver, and Evaluator all run their own inference evaluation logic independently. They share the same underlying shape — evaluate X against criteria, return verdict + rationale — but implement it separately. This creates drift and prevents the wrappers from benefiting from shared improvements.

Build EvaluatorCore as a shared module in devices/evaluator/core.py:
  evaluate(context: str, optimism: float, rubric: str | None) -> {verdict: pass/fail/partial, rationale: str, confidence: float}
  optimism=-1.0: bias toward finding what is wrong (Critic mode)
  optimism=0.0: neutral assessment (Judge mode)
  optimism=+1.0: bias toward what could be improved (Improver mode)

Refactor EvaluatorDevice (3-panel) to call EvaluatorCore 3x with slight variation and take majority verdict. This makes EvaluatorDevice a thin orchestrator over EvaluatorCore rather than containing its own inference logic.

**Affected files:** devices/evaluator/core.py (new); devices/evaluator/device.py (refactor to call core)
**Design rules:** no-autonomous-action; no SQLite; external-state; all inference via InferenceDevice
**Scope boundary:** IN: EvaluatorCore module; EvaluatorDevice refactored to use it. OUT: Critic/Improver wiring (separate tickets); rubric schema changes; optimism as a DB-stored parameter.
**Completion criteria:**
  1. EvaluatorCore.evaluate(context, optimism=-1.0) returns a verdict biased toward failure detection
  2. EvaluatorCore.evaluate(context, optimism=+1.0) returns a verdict biased toward improvement suggestions
  3. Same input with optimism=-1 vs +1 produces measurably different rationale content (verified by test)
  4. EvaluatorDevice calls EvaluatorCore 3x and returns majority verdict — existing eval_history tests pass
